_meta.purpose: Context handoff for OverGrown RPG Character Sheet project

stack.styling: Tailwind CSS v4 – @import 'tailwindcss' in globals.css; dark mode via @custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark, .dark *))
stack.state: Zustand v5 – devtools + persist, localStorage key: 'overgrown-character'

file_tree.src/types/game.ts: ALL type definitions (AttributeName, MasteryLevel, DerivedStats, Spell, CharacterAttack, InventoryItem, ItemEffect, Character, etc.)
file_tree.src/features/character/store/characterSt: Zustand store – all actions, persist merge, constants
file_tree.src/features/character/components/Charac: Header bar: name/player/race/origin, resource inputs, import/export/rest
file_tree.src/features/character/components/Attrib: SVG pentagram attribute controls with item bonus display
file_tree.src/features/character/components/SkillL: Perícias grid with budget bar, mastery buttons, origin/item highlights, search
file_tree.src/features/character/components/Invent: Inventory item cards – equip, quantity, use, effect labels
file_tree.src/features/character/components/modals: Modal to add a custom spell with levels table
Modal to add a combat attack
Modal to add inventory item; supports 7 effect types including skillBonus/skillUnlock
Modal for potion use – manual roll input per heal/restoreIep effect
file_tree.src/layouts/RootLayout.tsx: App shell with dark mode toggle; renders <Outlet />
file_tree.src/pages/HomePage.tsx: Page layout: CharacterHeader → AttributePentagon → [SpellList, AttackList] → SkillList → Inventory
file_tree.src/styles/globals.css: Tailwind v4 import + dark mode custom variant

types.DerivedStats: { iep: number; pc: number; vida: number; resistencia: number; esquiva: number }

ItemEffect.optional_fields: attribute?: AttributeName | stat?: keyof DerivedStats | skillId?: string | value?: number | description?: string

types.InventoryItem: { id, name, description, quantity, type: ItemType, effects: ItemEffect[], equipped?: boolean }

Character.fields: id, name, playerName, race, level, divinity, attributes: Attributes, acquiredTalents: string[], customSpells: CustomSpell[], characterAttacks: CharacterAttack[], inventory: InventoryItem[], skills: Record<string, MasteryLevel>, origin?: string, currentResources: { vida, iep, pc }

passive_item_bonus_logic.attributeBonus: Added to attribute BEFORE formula (boosts vida/iep/etc)
passive_item_bonus_logic.statBonus: Added AFTER formula directly to derived stat

skill_system.mastery_cost: I=1pt total, II=3pt total, III=5pt total, IV=7pt total – acquiring Mastery I costs 1pt, each upgrade step (I→II, II→III, III→IV) costs 2pt. Origin skill's I is free; upgrades cost 2pt each (origin total: II=2, III=4, IV=6)
skill_system.origin_skill: Selected origin grants 1 skill at minimum Mastery I; that first I doesn't consume budget

item_skill_effects.skillBonus: Adds flat test bonus to the skill (shown as +X badge in SkillList)
item_skill_effects.skillUnlock: Forces skill to minimum Mastery I regardless of budget (sky-blue highlight)

skills_data.interface: SkillDefinition { id, name, description, requiresTraining: boolean }

notes.setAttribute: Clamps currentResources to new max after attribute change
notes.useItem: Applies fixed effect.value directly; decrements quantity by 1
notes.useItemWithValues: Applies user-provided roll values; decrements quantity by 1; called by UseItemModal

ui_color_coding.amber: equipped item border highlight / standard mastery button active state
ui_color_coding.violet: origin skill row background + ★ Origem badge + origin preview in CharacterHeader
ui_color_coding.sky_blue: item-unlocked skill row background + ⬡ Item badge
ui_color_coding.red: budget overflow warning / attribute point deficit

fields: Origem (select → 27 options, shows '→ SkillName' preview in violet)

AttributePentagon.item_bonus_display: Shows total = base + item in circle; +X item tag in violet below vertex when > 0
AttributePentagon.note: getItemAttrBonuses() helper reads active attributeBonus item effects

SkillList.budget_bar: spent / budget with red overflow text
SkillList.mastery_buttons: 5 buttons per skill: — I II III IV; disabled if over budget or above divinity cap
SkillList.origin_skill: violet bg, ★ Origem badge, cannot go below Mastery I
SkillList.item_unlocked_skill: sky-blue bg, ⬡ Item badge, cannot go below Mastery I
SkillList.item_bonus: +X⬡ badge shown; added to displayed total roll bonus
SkillList.layout: Two-column responsive grid + text search filter

Inventory.item_card: name, description, type badge, quantity +/-, equip toggle (amber border when equipped)

AddItemModal.bug_fix: Effect type select resets defaults on type change to avoid silent skillId/stat=undefined

UseItemModal.behavior: Shows a number input for each heal/restoreIep effect; calls useItemWithValues with the entered values

tailwind_notes.class_application: Add/remove .dark class on <html> – handled by useDarkMode hook

completed_systems: Character header (name, player, race, origin select, resources)
Attribute pentagon (SVG, point allocation, item bonus display)
Derived stats with passive item bonus (attributeBonus + statBonus)
Custom spell management (add/edit/remove, full level table)
Inventory (add/remove, equip toggle, quantity, potion use modal)
Item effects: heal, restoreIep, attributeBonus, statBonus, skillBonus, skillUnlock, custom
Perícias (Skills) system: 30 skills, mastery buttons, budget bar
27 Origins – free Mastery I for origin skill (not in budget)
Mastery caps: II max until div 20, III max until div 40, IV at div 40+
Item-driven skill effects: bonus (+X) and unlock (forced Mastery I)
